commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content:
Nor. Protestants, "No, but he would put strength in me. "He would enable me to stand my trial. (Haydock)
He would lay no falsehood to my charge.

commentaries.author: Gregory The Dialogist
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh Will he plead against me with his great power. No; but he would put strength in me. For the soul of one however righteous, if he be judged with strictness by Almighty God, is borne down by the weight of His mightiness. In which same words this is likewise to be understood, that whereas the holy man shews the might of God, what else of Him does he desire, but His weakness. And it is written, the weakness of God is stronger than men.

methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links

advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
